record.field: Ryot (Roll Your Own Tracker) is a self-hosted, all-in-one platform for tracking everything you consume and accomplish, from the books you read to the workouts you complete.

Ryot (Roll Your Own Tracker) is a self-hosted, all-in-one platform for tracking everything you consume and accomplish, from the books you read to the workouts you complete.

Ryot unifies media consumption and fitness tracking in a single, lightweight application with a clean interface and rich statistics. It covers books, movies, TV shows, video games, anime, manga, comics, podcasts, music and audiobooks, alongside workouts and body measurements. Built with Rust and TypeScript, it is notably memory-efficient and runs comfortably on resource-constrained hardware while integrating with services like Jellyfin, Plex, Kodi, Trakt and Goodreads.

Key features
  • Track many media types: books, shows, movies, games, anime, manga, comics, music, podcasts and audiobooks
  • Built-in fitness and health tracking for workouts and body measurements
  • Importers and live integrations (Jellyfin, Plex, Kodi, Trakt, Goodreads, Komga and more)
  • Insightful dashboards, summaries and progress statistics with smart collections
  • OpenID Connect (OIDC) authentication and a GraphQL API for automation
  • Lightweight footprint, Progressive Web App support and Docker Compose deployment
Best for

Self-hosting enthusiasts and privacy-conscious users who want a single, data-owned place to log and visualize their media consumption and fitness activity, replacing scattered tracking services and SaaS apps.

services: ryot-app: image: ignisda/ryot:v7.10 environment: - DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:{{ postgres_password }}@ryot-db:5432/postgres - SERVER_ADMIN_ACCESS_TOKEN={{ admin_access_token }} - TZ=UTC depends_on: ryot-db: condition: service_healthy restart: always pull_policy: always container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8000' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network ryot-db: image: postgres:16-alpine volumes: - ./ryot_postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data environment: - POSTGRES_PASSWORD={{ postgres_password }} - POSTGRES_USER=postgres - POSTGRES_DB=postgres - TZ=UTC healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -U postgres interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 restart: unless-stopped container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_ryot_db' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T ryot-db sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d ryot-db for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T ryot-db sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T ryot-db sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T ryot-db sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
